title: Projects
text:
arXiv Preprint | Code
Over the centuries, I have devoted countless hours to deciphering and translating the ancient scripts and dialects of the various peoples of Middle-earth. My goal is to gain a deeper understanding of the cultures and histories of these peoples by studying their languages. Currently, I am working on a monograph that explores the linguistic roots of the Elvish languages. Through extensive research and analysis, I hope to shed light on the connections between the different dialects of Elvish and their origins. This project has been particularly challenging, as Elvish is a complex and nuanced language, but I am determined to see it through to completion.

title: Projects
section: The History of the War of the Ring
text:
The History of the War of the Ring

arXiv Preprint | Code
I am creating a comprehensive and detailed history of the conflict that goes beyond the surface-level events. By gathering information from a variety of sources, including my own memories, written accounts, and oral histories, I hope to shed new light on this important period in Middle-earth’s history and provide valuable insights into the motivations and actions of the various players involved.

title: SisengAI
text:

Hello, World!

Thanks for dropping by. My name is Ricky, and SisengAI is a pet project that showcases my venture in to Data Science, Machine Learning, Financial Engineering and NLP. I’m currently a data science instructor at WorldQuant University located in New Orleans, LA. We also offer a free Master in Financial Engineering at the same university. Follow the links I have shared to enroll in any of them if you are interested.



education


MSc in Financial Engineering, 2021


WorldQuant University, New Orleans, LA, USA.


BS in Electrical & Electronics Engineering, 2000


Abubakar Tafawa Balewa University, Bauchi, Nigeria.




experience


Data Science Instructor, 2022-present


WorldQuant University


Co-author and member of Masakhane, since 2020



Participatory Research for Low-resourced Machine Translation: A Case Study in African Languages




AfriMTE and AfriCOMET: Empowering COMET to Embrace Under-resourced African Languages



On this website, you will find a selection of my published works, blog posts. You will also find news and updates on my current research projects, as well as information on upcoming events and conferences where I will be speaking. I invite you to explore my website and learn more about my works. Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or would like to discuss potential projects.
